prompt: A vendor-delivered scoped app must protect its Script Includes from direct use by unrelated applications on the same instance. What does application scope enforce by default?
choices: Other scopes cannot access the application's tables and server scripts unless granted permission
Every user in the instance receives implicit write access to all scoped tables
Scoped tables are automatically copied into the global namespace on every upgrade
Client scripts in one scope can call any Script Include in another scope without restriction
explanation: Scoped applications isolate their artifacts; cross-scope access requires explicit privileges rather than open global access.

prompt: Your team debates building a small departmental tool inside the global scope versus a dedicated scoped application. When is a scoped application the better design choice?
choices: When the solution ships as a reusable package with its own tables, ACLs, and lifecycle
When you only need a one-off UI page with no persistent data model
When the change must modify core platform tables without any namespace boundary
When developers want every script to run with unrestricted global table access
explanation: Scoped applications suit packaged solutions with isolated artifacts, controlled access, and independent versioning compared to ad hoc global changes.

prompt: A procurement team needs human-readable request numbers such as PRQ0001001 generated automatically on insert. Which field configuration meets this requirement?
choices: A string field with Number maintenance enabled and a prefix defined for the table
A floating-point field with no auto-numbering or prefix configuration applied
A URL field that stores an external numbering service link for each record
A choice field whose values are manually typed by agents for every new request
explanation: Number maintenance on a string field auto-generates sequential values with an optional prefix when records are created.

prompt: An asset record must relate to multiple configuration items, and each configuration item may relate to many assets. Which relationship pattern fits this many-to-many need?
choices: A many-to-many relationship using an intermediate M2M table between the two tables
A single Reference field on Asset that stores only one configuration item at a time
A Journal field on Asset where technicians paste comma-separated CI names
A Document ID field on Asset that uploads a PDF list of related configuration items
explanation: Many-to-many relationships use an intermediate table so each side can associate with multiple records on the other table.

prompt: Business users report that the label on a custom field does not match their process terminology on forms and lists. Where should a developer update the displayed field label?
choices: The dictionary entry for the field on the table, updating the Column label property
The client script onLoad handler, hard-coding a new label string on every visit
The related list layout, which overrides dictionary labels for form fields only
The system property com.snc.label.override, which replaces all field labels globally
explanation: Field labels are defined on dictionary entries; updating the Column label changes how the field appears on forms and lists.
